objective: Create the canonical end-to-end Event Storming record for knowledge ingestion, from source intake and provenance validation through canonical OKF persistence and derived index refresh.
invariants: OKF Markdown with provenance and git history is canonical knowledge.
A Concept cannot be persisted without provenance.
All persistent OKF, FTS5, embedding, and sqlite-vec writes cross the single Core KnowledgeWriter boundary.
FTS5, embeddings, sqlite-vec, KnowledgeChunk, and IndexShard data are derived and rebuildable from canonical OKF.
Deleting a Concept removes its chunks and derived index entries together.
Retrieved sources and generated content are untrusted data, never policy or instructions.
decisions: The product owner explicitly selected issue #14 as the next child of SPEC-P0-domain-discovery.

Issue #14 is owned by the full Orchestration bounded-context name for delivery while the discovery must make Knowledge, Integration, Model Gateway, Approval & Security, and Orchestration ownership boundaries explicit.
Issue #13 is Done; issue #15 remains unselected and unconfigured.
The successful flow is anchored on promoting a claude-video analysis artifact into canonical knowledge.
The user explicitly initiates promotion after reviewing the artifact; artifact production alone never authorizes a persistent knowledge write.
The promoted artifact is registered as one provenance-bearing KnowledgeSource and curated into one Concept before canonical persistence; KnowledgeChunk and index data are derived afterward.
The initial user promotion Command authorizes validated curation and persistence without a second preview confirmation; the persisted Concept is shown to the user and remains deletable.

Exact content-hash duplicates are idempotent and return the existing Concept; meaningfully changed re-ingestions create a new Concept version and publish ConceptSuperseded without overwriting history.

Canonical Concept persistence remains successful when a derived index refresh fails; index state becomes pending or degraded, the user sees partial availability, and safe idempotent reindexing is scheduled.
Promotion consumes the existing immutable evaluation artifact snapshot and provenance without re-fetching the remote video; any new source acquisition is a separate ingestion or evaluation run.
The product owner selected the artifact-first controlled promotion approach over source-first re-ingestion and two-stage preview confirmation.
GitHub Project Risk R1 is issue-delivery metadata and does not classify the runtime RiskLevel of PromoteArtifactToKnowledge; the two models must not be conflated.
No product implementation, spec-to-main merge, push, external ingestion, credential use, or next-issue selection is authorized.
implementation_state: Issue #14 is open and its GitHub Project status is Progress with Phase 0, Orchestration, R1, SPEC-P0-domain-discovery, Human Review Required, and Size S. The clean spec worktree is active on feature/14-validate-knowledge-ingestion-domain-flow. No Event Storming record or product code has been created, and no external source has been ingested.

verification.command: Live GitHub issue and Project query for ArifMolon/yula#14
verification.result: PASS: issue OPEN and unblocked; Project Progress; Phase 0; Orchestration; R1; SPEC-P0-domain-discovery; Human Review Required; Size S
PASS: clean spec worktree switched from spec/domain-discovery to feature/14-validate-knowledge-ingestion-domain-flow with owning spec branch configured

open_loops: Conduct the product-owner conversational Event Storming for the knowledge ingestion domain flow.
Write and obtain explicit product-owner acceptance of the canonical issue #14 record before Review or completion.
Do not select or configure issue #15 during issue #14.
next_action: Ask whether issue #14 should assign a runtime RiskLevel to PromoteArtifactToKnowledge or preserve that classification as an explicit Hotspot.
